title: INVITRO-MICROPROPAGATION OF VIRUS-FREE GUAVA (Psidium guajava L.) PLANTLETS THROUGH MERISTEM CULTURE TECHNIQUE
author: Benisheikh, A.A G
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Standardization of protocol for in vitro cloning of virus-free microprogation of Guava plantlets through meristem culture was carried out using the stock plant of guava maintained at shade net house (75% shade and pruned severely to encourage new growth about 3cm long shoots were excited and defoliated. The explants were pretreated in solution containing 0.1% carbendazine and 100mg/L PVP for 1 hour and then washed with Twenty-20 wetting agent. The pretreated explants were further treated with Hgcl 0.1% for 5 minutes aseptically followed by thorough washing in autoclaved distilled water. The sterilized explants were cultured on MS medium supplemented with BAP (3.0 mg/L) for shoot bud induction and proliferation. The proliferated shoots were then sub cultured on MS medium containing 10mg/L IBA for rhizogesis. The rooted plants were finally shifted to autoclaved coconut husk fortified with ½ MS salt mixture for acclimatization. The in vitro plantlets after rooting were acclimatized in jiffy pots and established in the soil.

keywords: Invitro, micropropagation, virus-free, guava, plantlets, meristem culture, technique.
